title: A First-Timer's Guide to the Passover Seder
description: Everything you need to run or attend your first Seder — from the Seder plate to the Four Questions to finding the afikoman.
chinese_cultural_parallels: Dragon Boat Festival (端午节) - commemorative foods like zongzi
Mid-Autumn Festival (中秋节) - mooncakes symbolizing family reunion
Confucian filial piety (孝, xiào) - remembering ancestors through shared ritual
Shang Dynasty (商朝) - historical context for Abraham and the Patriarchs

chinese_historical_context.temple_period: Spring and Autumn period (春秋时代, 957-586 BCE)
chinese_historical_context.hillel_period: Han Dynasty (汉朝, 1st century BCE to 1st century CE)

key_concepts: 15 steps of the Seder (from Kadesh to Chad Gadya)
Six symbolic foods on the Seder plate
Four cups of wine representing four expressions of redemption
Matzah and maror as Torah-commanded foods
Afikoman as the final food eaten
Reclining as a symbol of freedom
target_audience: Complete beginners attending or hosting their first Passover Seder, non-Jews invited to a Seder, potential converts, Chinese readers with no Jewish background
tone: Warm, educational, practical, culturally sensitive, encouraging
special_features: Step-by-step walkthrough of all 15 Seder stages
FAQ section for common beginner questions
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
header_image_prompt: A serene Chinese brushstroke (shan shui) style landscape featuring a family gathered around a low table set with symbolic foods, rendered in soft ink wash with warm sepia and gold accents. In the background, misty mountains suggest the journey from slavery to freedom. Elements include: a traditional wine cup, unleavened bread, spring greenery, and an open book with Hebrew text, all integrated into a harmonious composition that blends Jewish and East Asian artistic sensibilities. The mood is warm, contemplative, and inviting — capturing both the solemnity and joy of the Passover celebration.
